commits.final_notes: Only commit in f2466c9..c9a7623 that changes benchmark/hybrid/server retrieval path. It adds opt-in telemetry wrappers and response-shape unwrapping but preserves the preexisting all-expanded-query loop, RECALL_TOP_K=10, candidate accumulation, dedupe, and ANSWER_CONTEXT_TOP_K=15 path when telemetry is off. Because later telemetry-off Regime C is high-context-ish and benchmark source hash remains identical from cb56559 through c9a7623/662514a, cb56559 cannot by static evidence explain A low-context vs C high-context as a telemetry-on perturbation. It is the smallest code-change boundary to test only if checkpoint execution is later authorized.

commits.subject: docs: record LOCOMO telemetry instrumentation window
commits.final_notes: Adds LOCOMO telemetry mutation-window helper script and docs/results artifacts. It does not change target benchmark/server/hybrid source. The script can perform read-only snapshots/probes when invoked but is not part of benchmark runtime path.

benchmark_path_preexisting_at_f2466c9: f2466c9 benchmark already has USE_QUERY_EXPANSION gating expand_query(question).
f2466c9 non-legacy path loops over all queries returned by expand_query(question).
f2466c9 only breaks early when not USE_QUERY_EXPANSION; with query expansion enabled it accumulates recalls across expanded queries.
f2466c9 uses RECALL_TOP_K/candidate_recall_top_k per query and then candidates[:ANSWER_CONTEXT_TOP_K].
cb56559_source_effect: Introduces _extract_recall_payload() to unwrap list or {results, memories, telemetry}.
Introduces recall_for_query() wrapper that optionally adds include_telemetry and then returns recalled results.
Replaces duplicated recall payload/unwrapping code with recall_for_query() in both legacy and non-legacy branches.
Adds recall_telemetry counters/projections and optional row[recall_telemetry] output.
Adds HybridRetriever include_telemetry parameter; when false, return path remains final_results list.
Server returns legacy list shape unless include_telemetry is true; include_telemetry true returns {results, telemetry}.
post_cb56559_stability: benchmark_scripts/locomo_bench_v2.py hash is identical at cb56559, 5cb6f0b, c9a7623, and 662514a.
hybrid_retrieval.py hash is identical at cb56559, 5cb6f0b, c9a7623, and 662514a.
server.py changes at 5cb6f0b are serialization-only; after 5cb6f0b server.py hash is identical through c9a7623/662514a.

conclusion.culprit_range: f2466c9..cb56559 effective runtime/source boundary, with cb56559 as only committed target-source change but not a specific static culprit
conclusion.confidence: medium for range, low for specific commit causality
conclusion.why_not_specific_commit: The all-expanded-query accumulation mechanism existed at f2466c9 source, so static committed source alone cannot explain f2466c9 low-context output. cb56559 is the only target-source change, but its visible changes are telemetry wrappers/observation plumbing and preserve telemetry-off list behavior. Therefore the strongest conclusion is an effective source/runtime/artifact boundary rather than a proven code-line culprit.
conclusion.recommended_next: Step 5o separately preregistered bounded checkpoint/trace-lite reproduction, not full 199Q first; compare f2466c9 effective source vs cb56559/current with fixed adversarial and non-adversarial rows and trace-lite counts.
